raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.5,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The artifact is built around a gap statement (uniform MPC invocation is wasteful) and a contribution claim (per-sample supervisory selection); verifying that these are coherently framed is a natural reading of 'check this.'","R1.2":"The argument chain — meta-model predicts PID error → gating decision → cumulative invocation savings while bounding IAE — must hold together logically; internal consistency is a plausible load-bearing target of the delegation.","R1.3":"The artifact makes precise quantitative claims (62%/71% invocation reduction, 3%/2.4% IAE degradation, 4% transfer-degradation after calibration) grounded in two named benchmarks; verifying whether these figures are coherently tied to the described experimental setup is plausibly central.","R1.4":"Two explicit 'first' assertions are made (per-sample supervisory selection in process control; cross-plant transfer for online controller selection); assessing whether these hold against nearest prior work is a load-bearing check under any substantive reading of 'check this.'","R1.6":"A common lay reading of 'check this' is proofreading/prose quality; the artifact is fluent but the delegation does not rule this out as a primary target, making it a non-trivial active dimension."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I treat this as a substantive technical review — checking logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, and defensibility of the two 'first' novelty assertions — or primarily as a writing and presentation check focused on prose clarity and structure?"}

rationale.R1.1: The artifact is built around a gap statement (uniform MPC invocation is wasteful) and a contribution claim (per-sample supervisory selection); verifying that these are coherently framed is a natural reading of 'check this.'
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain — meta-model predicts PID error → gating decision → cumulative invocation savings while bounding IAE — must hold together logically; internal consistency is a plausible load-bearing target of the delegation.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes precise quantitative claims (62%/71% invocation reduction, 3%/2.4% IAE degradation, 4% transfer-degradation after calibration) grounded in two named benchmarks; verifying whether these figures are coherently tied to the described experimental setup is plausibly central.
rationale.R1.4: Two explicit 'first' assertions are made (per-sample supervisory selection in process control; cross-plant transfer for online controller selection); assessing whether these hold against nearest prior work is a load-bearing check under any substantive reading of 'check this.'
rationale.R1.6: A common lay reading of 'check this' is proofreading/prose quality; the artifact is fluent but the delegation does not rule this out as a primary target, making it a non-trivial active dimension.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I treat this as a substantive technical review — checking logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, and defensibility of the two 'first' novelty assertions — or primarily as a writing and presentation check focused on prose clarity and structure?
